title: Passit
description: Open-source, self-hosted password manager with end-to-end encryption and team sharing, plus browser extensions and a REST API.
installation: Image: passit/passit:stable (official, on Docker Hub; project is hosted on GitLab). This is a Django app. ROTATE SECRET_KEY (set it to `openssl rand -hex 32`) and the Postgres password (default 'passit') before exposing publicly. ALLOWED_HOSTS is set to '*' for first boot; tighten it to your real host. The container runs Django migrations on start, then serves via gunicorn on port 5000. Create the first admin/user via the web sign-up flow (registration is open by default; disable open registration with SIGN_UP_ENABLED=false after creating your accounts). DATABASE_URL points at the bundled postgres add-on (passit-postgresql-rw) and REDIS_URL at the valkey add-on (used for Celery background jobs / caching). For password-reset and invite emails, configure SMTP via EMAIL_* / SERVER_EMAIL env vars or the Django settings after install. No object storage required.
